response.description: Agent-facing assistance interface for Freeq client validation and diagnostic queries. Returns conclusions, never raw state.

diagnosis.summary: Client configuration looks compatible with current server expectations.

safe_facts: Capability bitmap observed: message-tags=true, server-time=true, batch=true, sasl=true, resume=false, e2ee=false, echo-message=true, away-notify=true.

diagnosis.summary: Session state reported. Cross-check against what your client thinks it has — drift is the most common bug-source for long-running bots.

safe_facts: Negotiated capabilities: message-tags, server-time, batch, echo-message, account-notify, extended-join, away-notify, multi-prefix

suggested_fixes.summary: If this is a bot/agent, declare actor class via `+freeq.at/actor-class=agent`.
suggested_fixes.details: Other clients flag undeclared agents that look like humans. Set the cap via your IRCv3 message-tags during JOIN.

followups.reason: Use this if the session count looks wrong or the bot was dropped recently.

diagnosis.summary: A PRIVMSG to `#dev` from `did:key:z6Mkodd1y61yrSjvte7Vh3utJtWM2KvrbJiBGr1MPC7UabBW` should be accepted.

safe_facts: Sender `did:key:z6Mkodd1y61yrSjvte7Vh3utJtWM2KvrbJiBGr1MPC7UabBW` has 1 live session(s); best one has 0 send(s) in the last 2s window (limit 5 / 2s, so 5 send(s) of headroom).

followups.reason: Use this if the prediction was NOT_IN_CHANNEL and you can't JOIN.

diagnosis.summary: Parsed `PRIVMSG` with target `#dev` — see facts for routing implications.

safe_facts: Buffer to route into: `#dev` (bot logic should display the message there).
Self-echo: this is your own message reflected back via echo-message. Skip it in your reply logic, otherwise you'll loop.
